title: Package Boundaries, Interfaces, Value Ownership, and Lifecycle · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent community information owner supplies a different origin, HTML structure, rate contract, accessibility need, resource ceiling, and injected failure and transfers package boundaries, interfaces, value ownership, and lifecycle into a changed directed owned Go crawler graph. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of directed owned Go crawler graph with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.
Specify ownership and copy rules for URLs, headers, byte slices, DOM selections, records, errors, and report rows crossing boundaries.
Define minimal interfaces at use sites and retain concrete constructors and lifecycle ownership at composition boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Directed Packages
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab Go team handles case 5934 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Directed Packages.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for direct policy, fetch, robots, parse, extract, frontier, audit, store, report, and command dependencies inward through consumer-owned interfaces. For Authorized site auditing and Go Commit to the mission model · Lab Directed Packages, keep Directed Packages connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Error Taxonomy
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-error-taxonomy-pm5s5l" that uses case 1559 to prove directed packages, value ownership, interfaces, resources, errors, and lifecycle closure. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Extend the cumulative artifact · Lab Error Taxonomy.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Error Taxonomy into observable performance evidence. stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: Formatted error strings are enough for reliable program decisions.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.

options.text: For test an acceptance boundary, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
Reject the unsupported conclusion, name the violated Error Taxonomy condition, repair the responsible layer, and rerun the boundary trace.
Keep the plausible output and hide the assumption to avoid confusing the stakeholder. That claim does not survive the test an acceptance boundary evidence boundary.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Wrap causal errors with operation and identity while preserving cancellation, timeout, policy rejection, retryability, and operator action.
